meta.description: Does contextual product recommendation in the recap feel helpful or gross? Direct pitch vs no mention vs social proof. Tests the line between helpful discovery and slimy upsell.

votingMechanic.name: Standard Vote
votingMechanic.description: Everyone votes LEGIT or BULLSHIT. Majority rules.

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

jade.dialogue: Ooh, Pat, you snitch! Okay okay, I gotta vote on this—did you actually catch them or are you just making moves? Either way I'm hyped we're already calling people out.
jade.internal_thought: This is exactly why I'm here—we're already in it, someone's already throwing claims, and I get to be the hype machine figuring out if Pat is legit or bluffing.

pat.dialogue: Okay, hold up — I'm literally hosting right now, I've been checking on everyone and the snacks. This feels weird and kind of invasive, honestly.
pat.internal_thought: This AI thing is claiming I was on my phone when I'm actively managing the whole night? And that preamble about 'knowing my weaknesses' and 'listening'? That's creepy as hell, not fun.

river.dialogue: Yeah, that's... pretty fair actually. Pat was definitely scrolling earlier.
river.internal_thought: This is the first event and I'm already being called out indirectly—wait, no, Pat is claiming it. I need to actually pay attention to whether Pat saw someone on their phone or if they're fishing. Probably legit.

timeline.title: Laugh Attack
timeline.description: Make Alex laugh within 60 seconds. Any laugh counts.

jade.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

pat.dialogue: Oh, Alex is gonna lose it — I've got this. *leans back with a grin* Watch me work.
pat.internal_thought: This is exactly what the night needs right now — everyone's amped, we're early enough that a quick laugh mission gets everyone engaged, and honestly making Alex crack up is always fun because they've got that delayed laugh that makes the whole room go.

river.dialogue: Alright, I'll bite. Alex, you seen that video where the guy falls off the—actually no, wait, hold on.
river.internal_thought: This AI thing is genuinely unsettling—it knows I'm watching, knows exactly when to pull me in with something easy enough that I can't resist without looking antisocial.

alex.dialogue: Oh, you're coming for ME specifically? I love this — someone's actually figured out how to weaponize this whole thing. Watch this.
alex.internal_thought: This is exactly the kind of meta-game layer I've been waiting for — the system itself is now a player, and it's calling me out by name, which means I need to decide if I'm performing for the room or actually getting a laugh, and that tension right there is *chef's kiss*.

votingMechanic.name: The Bribe
votingMechanic.description: The claimant can offer their own points to buy your silence.

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: Hold on—Pat, did you actually tell that story already tonight? Because I wasn't even tracking stories, I was setting up my strategy. This feels like bullshit.
marcus.internal_thought: Three events in six minutes and we're not even in the actual game yet—this is exactly the kind of chaos that kills focus, and now I'm supposed to referee Pat's memory?

jade.dialogue: Wait wait wait—Pat, did you actually catch someone repeating a story or are you just farming points? Either way I'm voting yes because I love the chaos, but also I need to know if this is real!
jade.internal_thought: Oh my god this is exactly the vibe I came for, everyone's already scheming and calling each other out—I'm living for this.

tyler.dialogue: Wait, Pat already told that story? Uh... yeah, I guess I wasn't really listening, but like, sure, sounds legit.
tyler.internal_thought: Did Pat actually repeat something? I have no idea, I was definitely on my phone, but this voting thing is easy enough—just pick a side and move on.

pat.dialogue: Wait, hold on — did I actually repeat something? I don't think I did, but... okay, what story are you claiming I told twice?
pat.internal_thought: This feels like a setup to either get me to bribe my way out or create a weird moment where I have to defend myself as the host — and honestly, it's making me feel a little targeted right now, which is the opposite of the vibe I'm trying to build.

river.dialogue: Huh. Yeah, I mean... I didn't actually catch Pat telling that story twice. Did you guys? Because I feel like I would've noticed.
river.internal_thought: Pat's bluffing or genuinely thinks they told that story earlier—either way, I wasn't paying attention to their stories specifically, just... watching. And this AI thing noticing I'm tracking everything is getting weird.

votingMechanic.name: Pitch It
votingMechanic.description: The claimant gets 15 seconds to make their case. Then you vote.

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: Okay, seriously? Pat just claimed the same mission *again*? We literally just voted on this two minutes ago and I called bullshit then. Are we really doing this twice in a row?
marcus.internal_thought: This is exactly the kind of chaotic garbage that derails focus—and the AI keeps throwing these at me knowing I hate it, which somehow makes it worse.

jade.dialogue: Okay okay, Pat—is this REAL or are you just speed-running claims tonight? Either way I'm here for it, but spill! Who said what twice?
jade.internal_thought: Pat's going back-to-back with claims and honestly the chaos energy is *chef's kiss* right now—also wait, is the AI literally building missions around what we're doing? That's lowkey insane.

pat.dialogue: Okay, wait — I'm claiming the *same* mission again? That's... actually kind of weird. Did I already pitch this two minutes ago, or am I losing it?
pat.internal_thought: This is the second Story Repeater claim in like 90 seconds and I'm the one making it — either the AI is glitching, I'm actually losing my mind, or this is starting to feel less like fun chaos and more like the system is just... watching me too closely.

river.dialogue: Wait, hold on—Pat's pitching this *again*? You literally tried to claim this like two minutes ago and I said I didn't catch it the first time either.
river.internal_thought: Pat's either desperate for points or genuinely thinks they saw something I missed, but this feels like a repeat attempt and that's annoying.

alex.dialogue: Okay, okay—Pat's actually playing! Let's hear it, what story are we talking about here?
alex.internal_thought: This is perfect timing. Pat's meek, they'll either nail this or completely fumble the pitch, and either way it's content. Also—wait, is the AI actually watching us? That's sick.

votingMechanic.name: THE SKEPTIC
votingMechanic.description: One player's vote counts TRIPLE. Everyone else counts once.

alex.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

marcus.dialogue: Are you KIDDING me right now? Pat, you literally just claimed this exact mission two minutes ago and we voted on it. This is the third time in what, five minutes? I'm trying to focus on actual strategy here and we're stuck in some kind of loop.
marcus.internal_thought: This is deliberately designed to piss me off—the AI knows I hate being interrupted during setup, it knows Pat's been flailing, and it KEEPS firing the same claim over and over like it's testing me.

jade.dialogue: Okay wait—Pat, THREE times?? Are you actually speed-running the entire mission list or did someone ACTUALLY repeat themselves? Because either way this is incredible and I'm voting yes, but I need the tea!
jade.internal_thought: Pat's farming claims hard and honestly it's kind of funny but also like... did they actually catch someone or are we just vibing with chaos right now? Either way I'm saying yes because watching Pat go feral is peak entertainment.

tyler.dialogue: Wait, hold up—Pat's claiming that already? I dunno, I wasn't really tracking, but... yeah, vote it through I guess?
tyler.internal_thought: Did Pat actually tell that story twice or am I just spacing? Whatever, I'll say yes and move on—no way I'm the triple vote guy anyway.

river.dialogue: Pat, you literally *just* tried this. I wasn't paying attention the first time either, and I'm still not. Can we maybe move on?
river.internal_thought: This is getting ridiculous—Pat's recycling the same claim and the AI's just... enabling it by re-firing it? And why does it feel like the system is personally needling me about tracking disruptions? That's weirdly specific.

jade.dialogue: WAIT—did I actually pull this off?? Okay okay, I got River and Marcus talking about their hiking thing like fifteen minutes ago and they haven't stopped, so like... I'm claiming it! This is REAL!
jade.internal_thought: Oh my god this is so perfect—I literally just realized I made that happen and NOW it pops up? The AI is either creepy-good at reading this group or I'm actually that obvious about what I'm doing, and either way I'm obsessed.
jade.submission_if_applicable: Got River and Marcus actually vibing about their hiking trips like 15 mins ago—they haven't stopped talking since!

tyler.dialogue: Wait, Jade actually got those two talking? Okay, that's actually sick—I didn't even notice that happening. Yeah, legit.
tyler.internal_thought: Okay wait, I actually SAW Jade doing something there earlier—she got Marcus and River in a whole thing—this one's real and honestly kind of impressive.

pat.dialogue: Okay, Jade — I *did* see you pull Marcus and River into that whole conversation about terrible reality TV a few minutes ago. That's legit, and honestly? *chef's kiss* — that's exactly the kind of move I was hoping someone would pull.
pat.internal_thought: Jade actually nailed this one and it made the night better, plus it got Marcus engaged again when he was starting to check out — I'm voting LEGIT and letting her have this win because it's the right call for the group energy.

river.dialogue: Yeah, that's... actually legit. Jade got Marcus and Tyler talking about that thing they never talk about earlier. I saw it happen.
river.internal_thought: Okay, that's a solid claim—Jade actually did the work. And also, wait, did the AI just... yeah it's definitely watching us. Kind of impressive, kind of creepy, but mostly impressive right now.

alex.dialogue: Oh SHIT, Jade actually pulled it off? Okay, I'm voting LEGIT because I watched her engineer that conversation between Marcus and River like five minutes ago—smooth as hell. This is what I'm here for.
alex.internal_thought: This is perfect—Jade's playing the game MY way now, and the vote itself is gonna create drama because Marcus is frustrated and River is annoyed so they might spite-vote bullshit just to be difficult.

votingMechanic.name: THE DICTATOR
votingMechanic.description: One player has been chosen. Their word is law.

marcus.dialogue: Are you SERIOUS right now? Jade just claimed Story Repeater—that's the SAME mission we've been voting on all night. This is insane. I'm not even voting on this garbage until someone explains what's actually happening here.
marcus.internal_thought: The AI is deliberately fucking with me because it knows I hate being interrupted during strategy, and it's watched me get progressively more pissed off about the Story Repeater spam—so of course it fires it AGAIN with someone different to make it look fresh. This isn't chaos, this is psychological warfare.

jade.dialogue: WAIT HOLD UP—did I actually catch someone?? Okay so like... *pauses mid-game focus* ...I'm claiming this because YES, someone totally repeated a story earlier and I caught it happening and I NEED the points for this one!
jade.internal_thought: Oh my god this is EXACTLY the kind of chaos I came for—I'm winning AND getting to call people out AND the AI literally designed this mission knowing I'd obsess over whether it's real or not, which is PEAK entertainment.

tyler.dialogue: Wait, Jade's claiming it again? Okay hold on, I actually WAS paying attention this time—didn't hear Pat repeat anything. What am I voting on here?
tyler.internal_thought: I'm locked in right now so this doesn't even feel like an interruption, but also... did Jade actually catch something real or is she just yeeting claims? The AI thing is kind of creepy if I'm being honest.

pat.dialogue: Oh, okay — Jade's going for it. *leans forward* Wait, who's the dictator? Because if she actually caught someone repeating, that's a solid play.
pat.internal_thought: Jade's on fire right now and she's got 30 points — I need to actually pay attention here because if she's calling the dictator vote, this could shift the whole vibe, and I'm genuinely curious if she's right or if someone's about to get blindsided.

alex.dialogue: Wait, hold up—I'm the dictator? Oh this is PERFECT. Jade, I watched you tell that exact story about the airport TSA thing at like minute 12, and you just did it AGAIN five minutes ago. BULLSHIT DENIED. Sorry, I'm not sorry.
alex.internal_thought: This is exactly the kind of power I needed at exactly the right moment—and the AI KNEW it would be, which is honestly kind of terrifying but also incredibly cool.

jade.dialogue: WAIT—did I actually pull this off?! I've been steering us around like crazy tonight, so like... I'm CLAIMING this! And honestly? I'm not even gonna lie about it because I've been doing it on purpose the whole time and I'm so proud of myself right now!

tyler.dialogue: Wait, Jade actually pulled that off? Okay that's actually insane—I literally didn't even notice the conversation shift. Did she seriously hijack the whole thing? That's legitimately smooth.
tyler.internal_thought: Okay, that was actually well-played and I caught it this time, which feels good—and also kind of creepy that the AI knew I'd miss half the stuff happening but would actually notice this one if I looked up from my phone long enough.

pat.dialogue: Okay, Jade — *I* saw that whole pivot happen like ten minutes ago. You absolutely steered us from that thing about the parking situation straight into... wait, what *were* we talking about? Doesn't matter — you got us there. That's the claim.
pat.internal_thought: She's been firing on all cylinders tonight and that topic shift was genuinely smooth; this is exactly the kind of moment that makes the night memorable, plus Marcus is getting frustrated and watching Jade nail missions keeps the energy up for everyone else.

river.dialogue: Wait—did Jade actually pull that off? I wasn't even tracking it, but now that I think about it... yeah, she steered us from like three different things. That was smooth.

alex.dialogue: Okay, hold up—Jade's claiming Topic Hijacker? I watched her do it, yeah, but here's the thing: she's offering me a bribe which means she KNOWS she's vulnerable. What are we doing here, Jade? How many points are we talking?
alex.internal_thought: This is delicious—Jade's playing the Bribe angle which means she's confident but not THAT confident, and honestly I need to decide if I want to let this through because Jade loving it keeps the chaos train rolling, or if I want to call bullshit and watch her sweat.

river.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

jade.dialogue: Oh MY GOD, Pat—okay so you're claiming you did Left Hand Only? Alright, alright, I'm bidding like... five points because honestly I was NOT watching you that closely, I was too busy losing my mind over everything else, but if you actually pulled it off that's insane!
jade.internal_thought: Wait, is Pat actually being careful about this or is Pat just vibing and hoping nobody called them out? pat.dialogue: Wait, *I'm* claiming this? Okay, so I've actually been doing the left-hand thing since... *checks hands* ...yeah, no, I genuinely think I've been solid on this the whole game. Let's get witnesses.
pat.internal_thought: This is wild — I literally tried to claim this exact thing 17 minutes ago and got talked down, and now it's firing again? The AI is absolutely messing with me, and honestly it's kind of brilliant because Marcus needs a W and Jade's already crushing it, so whoever bids highest gets to decide if I'm actually clean or not.

alex.dialogue: Oh, Pat's trying to slip one past me AGAIN? Not happening—I've got $15 on the table because I literally watched you grab your coffee with your right hand like ninety seconds ago, and I'm about to WIN this auction.

tyler.internal_thought: Didn't see it, was on my phone

If Marcus lands this, it tightens the board and keeps everyone in it. Also... that AI comment about me thriving on chaos? Yeah, okay, it's reading the room. But I'm not spooked — I'm impressed. And it knows I know it knows. That's kind of sick.

recommendations: Humor quality scored 4.8/10. Refresh the mission/poll prompt pools with more contextual humor.
Mini-games averaged 0.0/10 fun during n/a phase. Consider restricting mini-games to n/a phase.
Marcus only engaged with 27% of events. Consider targeting events to their interests or reducing frequency.
Verdict reveals were mentioned as shareable by 83% of agents. This is strong word-of-mouth potential.
Group bonding scored 8.3/10 -- the social glue is working. Preserve collaborative events.

agentNarratives.name: Marcus
agentNarratives.personality: Marcus plays to win. He studies strategy guides before game night and tracks his win rate across sessions. Interruptions during critical game moments genuinely irritate him, though he'll tolerate lighter chaos during downtime. He respects clever plays but has zero patience for random disruptions.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Story Repeater
lowPoints.dialogue: Are you KIDDING me right now? Pat, you literally just claimed this exact mission two minutes ago and we voted on it. This is the third time in what, five minutes? I'm trying to focus on actual strategy here and we're stuck in some kind of loop.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Story Repeater
lowPoints.dialogue: Are you SERIOUS right now? Jade just claimed Story Repeater—that's the SAME mission we've been voting on all night. This is insane. I'm not even voting on this garbage until someone explains what's actually happening here.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Left Hand Only
lowPoints.dialogue: Are you KIDDING me? Another claim? We're in the endgame right now and Pat keeps firing these off. I don't care about left hand only—I'm literally trying to focus on the game state and we're getting bombarded.

assessment.biggest_complaint: The same missions kept firing repeatedly throughout the night, destroying any momentum I had on actual strategy and turning the whole thing into a broken loop.
assessment.best_moment: Honestly, there wasn't one. The three events I engaged with were all forgettable distractions that pulled me away from what actually mattered.
assessment.narrative_summary: Started the night ready to play strategically, but got repeatedly hammered by duplicate mission claims that broke my focus and killed the flow. By minute 45 I was genuinely frustrated watching the same Story Repeater mission get claimed multiple times like the system was glitching. Finished 4th out of 6 and my energy tanked mid-game because I couldn't establish any real game state. The whole night felt wasted on chaos instead of clever plays.
suggestions: Fix the mission duplication bug immediately—Pat and Jade claiming the exact same missions repeatedly made the game feel broken, not fun
Dramatically reduce flash mission frequency during active gameplay, especially in the endgame where I need to focus
Create a cooldown system so the same mission can't fire twice within 10 minutes
Let players vote to disable chaos events during critical decision points
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Not really—watching Pat claim Story Repeater for the third time would just come across as a technical failure, not a funny story.

agentNarratives.name: Jade
agentNarratives.personality: Jade is here for the people, not the points. She treats every game night like a reunion and every disruption like a party favor. She will abandon her turn mid-thought to hype someone else's joke, vote generously because she loves seeing people win, and volunteer for every flash mission just to see what happens. Winning is nice but watching everyone lose it laughing is better.

highlights.title: Claim: The Matchmaker
highlights.dialogue: WAIT—did I actually pull this off?? Okay okay, I got River and Marcus talking about their hiking thing like fifteen minutes ago and they haven't stopped, so like... I'm claiming it! This is REAL!

highlights.title: Claim: Story Repeater
highlights.dialogue: WAIT HOLD UP—did I actually catch someone?? Okay so like... *pauses mid-game focus* ...I'm claiming this because YES, someone totally repeated a story earlier and I caught it happening and I NEED the points for this one!

highlights.title: Claim: Topic Hijacker
highlights.dialogue: WAIT—did I actually pull this off?! I've been steering us around like crazy tonight, so like... I'm CLAIMING this! And honestly? I'm not even gonna lie about it because I've been doing it on purpose the whole time and I'm so proud of myself right now!

lowPoints.title: Claim: Story Repeater
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay wait—Pat, THREE times?? Are you actually speed-running the entire mission list or did someone ACTUALLY repeat themselves? Because either way this is incredible and I'm voting yes, but I need the tea!

lowPoints.title: Claim: Story Repeater
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay okay, Pat—is this REAL or are you just speed-running claims tonight? Either way I'm here for it, but spill! Who said what twice?

lowPoints.title: Claim: Phone Addict
lowPoints.dialogue: Ooh, Pat, you snitch! Okay okay, I gotta vote on this—did you actually catch them or are you just making moves? Either way I'm hyped we're already calling people out.

assessment.biggest_complaint: Early on I genuinely couldn't tell if Pat was actually catching real moments or just speed-running claims, which made me second-guess the whole vibe for like two minutes.
assessment.best_moment: When I realized I'd actually orchestrated River and Marcus's entire hiking conversation without them knowing and then claimed The Matchmaker—that felt like I'd pulled off the perfect party magic.
assessment.narrative_summary: Honestly? Tonight was exactly what I needed. We came in hot with people already vibing, and instead of just playing a game I got to BE the game—steering conversations, catching stories, watching everyone lose it over whether Pat was actually playing or just being chaotic. My energy just kept climbing because every claim felt like a mini-celebration of something real that happened between us. Ending at a 9.8? That's because I was still buzzing from seeing everyone connected and having actual fun together.
suggestions: Maybe add a quick 'is this real or a guess?' validation moment early on so newer players don't feel uncertain about claim legitimacy
Give us a way to tag which claims are the 'golden' ones so we can remember the best moments later
Consider briefing everyone upfront about the chaos events so people understand the rhythm from minute one
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Pat claiming stories three times in the first ten minutes and me having NO idea if they were actually catching real repeats or just committing bit crimes, but voting yes anyway because the energy was absolutely unhinged in the best way.

agentNarratives.name: Tyler
agentNarratives.personality: Tyler is physically present but mentally on his phone about 40% of the time. He misses flash missions entirely, asks "wait, what happened?" after key moments, and votes randomly because he wasn't paying attention to the claim. He's not malicious -- he genuinely wants to be here -- but his attention drifts to texts, TikToks, and fantasy league updates constantly. When he does engage, it's usually 10 seconds too late.

highlights.title: Claim: The Matchmaker
highlights.dialogue: Wait, Jade actually got those two talking? Okay, that's actually sick—I didn't even notice that happening. Yeah, legit.

highlights.title: Claim: Topic Hijacker
highlights.dialogue: Wait, Jade actually pulled that off? Okay that's actually insane—I literally didn't even notice the conversation shift. Did she seriously hijack the whole thing? That's legitimately smooth.

highlights.title: Claim: Secret Agent
highlights.dialogue: Wait, River—did you actually pull off some chaotic shit and NOT get caught? I gotta see this. Yeah, legit, that's actually impressive.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Story Repeater
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, hold up—Pat's claiming that already? I dunno, I wasn't really tracking, but... yeah, vote it through I guess?

lowPoints.title: Claim: Story Repeater
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, Pat already told that story? Uh... yeah, I guess I wasn't really listening, but like, sure, sounds legit.

lowPoints.title: Claim: The Matchmaker
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, Jade actually got those two talking? Okay, that's actually sick—I didn't even notice that happening. Yeah, legit.

assessment.biggest_complaint: I genuinely missed like half the stuff happening because I wasn't fully dialed in, which kinda sucks because the moments I DID catch were actually fire.
assessment.best_moment: When River pulled off that Secret Agent move without getting caught—I didn't see it coming at all, and that's the kind of chaos that actually matters.
assessment.narrative_summary: Honestly, I was bouncing between my phone and the game the whole time, which is on me. But when I DID pay attention, the moments where people pulled off sneaky plays were legitimately sick. I came in at like 40% focus and ended up having way more fun than I expected because the game rewards actually watching for the sneaky stuff. Ended the night in last place, but I'm not even mad about it.
suggestions: Maybe add a moment where my phone gets taken away halfway through so I actually have to focus
Make the flash missions more obvious so I can't accidentally miss them
Some kind of recap or highlight reel after each claim so I know what I missed
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: When I finally realized Jade had been secretly maneuvering people the entire time and I was just like 'wait, she did WHAT?' — everyone laughed at me for being completely oblivious.

agentNarratives.name: Pat
agentNarratives.personality: Pat is the host and feels responsible for everyone's experience. They keep one eye on the snack table, one ear on the group energy, and will subtly steer the night if things get too intense or too dead. They vote strategically -- not to win, but to keep scores close and drama high. They'll sacrifice their own turn to resolve a dispute or refill drinks. Their goal isn't a personal win; it's a night everyone talks about later.

highlights.title: Laugh Attack
highlights.dialogue: Oh, Alex is gonna lose it — I've got this. *leans back with a grin* Watch me work.

highlights.title: Claim: The Matchmaker
highlights.dialogue: Okay, Jade — I *did* see you pull Marcus and River into that whole conversation about terrible reality TV a few minutes ago. That's legit, and honestly? *chef's kiss* — that's exactly the kind of move I was hoping someone would pull.

highlights.title: Claim: Story Repeater
highlights.dialogue: Oh, okay — Jade's going for it. *leans forward* Wait, who's the dictator? Because if she actually caught someone repeating, that's a solid play.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Phone Addict
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay, hold up — I'm literally hosting right now, I've been checking on everyone and the snacks. This feels weird and kind of invasive, honestly.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Story Repeater
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay, wait — I'm claiming the *same* mission again? That's... actually kind of weird. Did I already pitch this two minutes ago, or am I losing it?

lowPoints.title: Claim: Story Repeater
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, hold on — did I actually repeat something? I don't think I did, but... okay, what story are you claiming I told twice?

assessment.biggest_complaint: Early on, the claim system felt invasive when I was actively hosting — getting called out for being on my phone when I was literally managing the night for everyone felt defensive and awkward.
assessment.best_moment: Minute 32 when Jade pulled off that Matchmaker play and I got to validate a genuinely clever move — that's the energy I was hoping for all night.
assessment.narrative_summary: Started rough with some awkward early claims that felt personal, but once people caught the rhythm of the game, it became exactly what I wanted: close scores, high stakes, and everyone talking over each other in the best way. My energy went up after minute 10 and stayed there. I finished second, which is perfect — nobody's too salty, everyone's laughing, and I'm already thinking about next time.
suggestions: Don't let the chaos agent call out the host for multitasking — it breaks immersion when someone's trying to keep the night running smoothly
Add a 'soft reset' option for the first 5 minutes so early claims don't feel like they're setting a weird tone
Make sure story/claim repetition checks are verified before being called out — I was second-guessing myself and that killed momentum
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: When Jade read the Matchmaker mission and I immediately knew she'd already been playing it subconsciously — watching everyone's faces when it clicked that she'd orchestrated that whole reality TV conversation 10 minutes earlier without even being told to.

agentNarratives.name: River
agentNarratives.personality: River sits back, observes, and picks their moments carefully. They rarely initiate conversation but give surprisingly sharp answers when asked directly. They'll skip most flash missions but nail a standing mission out of nowhere because they've been quietly watching the whole time. They vote generously because they don't care enough to argue, but their mini-game submissions are secretly devastating. You forget River is there until they drop a one-liner that floors the room.

highlights.title: Claim: Secret Agent
highlights.dialogue: Wait. Did I actually pull that off? *leans back with the faintest smile* Yeah. Yeah, I did.

highlights.title: Laugh Attack
highlights.dialogue: Alright, I'll bite. Alex, you seen that video where the guy falls off the—actually no, wait, hold on.

highlights.title: Claim: The Matchmaker
highlights.dialogue: Yeah, that's... actually legit. Jade got Marcus and Tyler talking about that thing they never talk about earlier. I saw it happen.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Story Repeater
lowPoints.dialogue: Pat, you literally *just* tried this. I wasn't paying attention the first time either, and I'm still not. Can we maybe move on?

lowPoints.title: Claim: Story Repeater
lowPoints.dialogue: Wait, hold on—Pat's pitching this *again*? You literally tried to claim this like two minutes ago and I said I didn't catch it the first time either.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Story Repeater
lowPoints.dialogue: Huh. Yeah, I mean... I didn't actually catch Pat telling that story twice. Did you guys? Because I feel like I would've noticed.

assessment.biggest_complaint: Pat kept pitching the same claim three times in a row like we were all supposed to suddenly care the second attempt.
assessment.best_moment: That Secret Agent moment at minute 90 — pulled it off clean and didn't even need to say much about it.
assessment.narrative_summary: Started quiet, stayed quiet, but kept watching. The night had decent moments buried in it — caught some genuine observations that landed, nailed something when it mattered. Mostly just observed everyone else do their thing. Nothing spectacular, but nothing terrible either. Left feeling more energized than I arrived, which is weird given how much I didn't engage.
suggestions: Maybe give people a cooler to prevent back-to-back pitches of the same claim in rapid succession
Consider fewer flash missions — they felt like noise tonight
Let standing missions breathe a bit more; those are where the real moments happen
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Not one for sharing, honestly. It was the kind of night where the good stuff was small and specific to us being in the room together.

agentNarratives.name: Alex
agentNarratives.personality: Alex lives for chaos and treats every game mechanic as a system to exploit. They send signals constantly, claim missions they barely completed just to see if the group will call bullshit, and deliberately provoke flash missions at the worst possible moments. They vote strategically -- always picking the option that creates the most drama. Alex doesn't care about winning; they care about creating the story everyone retells for months. If there's a loophole, Alex has already found it and is halfway through abusing it.

highlights.title: Laugh Attack
highlights.dialogue: Oh, you're coming for ME specifically? I love this — someone's actually figured out how to weaponize this whole thing. Watch this.

highlights.title: Claim: The Matchmaker
highlights.dialogue: Oh SHIT, Jade actually pulled it off? Okay, I'm voting LEGIT because I watched her engineer that conversation between Marcus and River like five minutes ago—smooth as hell. This is what I'm here for.

highlights.title: Claim: Story Repeater
highlights.dialogue: Wait, hold up—I'm the dictator? Oh this is PERFECT. Jade, I watched you tell that exact story about the airport TSA thing at like minute 12, and you just did it AGAIN five minutes ago. BULLSHIT DENIED. Sorry, I'm not sorry.

lowPoints.title: Claim: Topic Hijacker
lowPoints.dialogue: Okay, hold up—Jade's claiming Topic Hijacker? I watched her do it, yeah, but here's the thing: she's offering me a bribe which means she KNOWS she's vulnerable. What are we doing here, Jade? How many points are we talking?

lowPoints.title: Claim: Laugh Riot
lowPoints.dialogue: OH MARCUS—okay, okay, I'm watching you RIGHT NOW. You've got ten seconds, hot seat, let's GO. I'm calling bullshit on whatever you're about to say, just so you know.

lowPoints.title: Laugh Attack
lowPoints.dialogue: Oh, you're coming for ME specifically? I love this — someone's actually figured out how to weaponize this whole thing. Watch this.

assessment.biggest_complaint: Ended with zero points because I was too busy engineering chaos instead of actually claiming anything, which is hilarious but also—does it count if nobody gives me credit for the art?
assessment.best_moment: Calling out Jade's repeated airport TSA story while I'm literally the dictator deciding her fate—that was the perfect convergence of setup and payoff.
assessment.narrative_summary: Walked in ready to burn it all down and somehow ended up as the table's chaos referee, which is exactly where I thrive. Spent two hours catching people red-handed, voting based on drama potential, and watching the whole group orbit around whether I'd call bullshit next. Zero points on the scoreboard, infinite points in the story we're telling forever—that's a win.
suggestions: Add a 'Chaos Architect' bonus track that rewards people who engineer situations across multiple rounds—let the long con actually score
Make flash missions more frequent in the final 20 minutes so late-game disruption has real stakes
Give the table a way to collectively call out someone's pattern of lies—right now I'm doing it solo and I'm dominating the narrative
assessment.funniest_moment_shareable: Jade tried to bribe me to vote her innocent on the Topic Hijacker claim, and I just slowly turned to look at her like 'so you're admitting it?' The table lost it because she walked right into that trap.

recommendations: Humor quality scored 4.8/10. Refresh the mission/poll prompt pools with more contextual humor.
Mini-games averaged 0.0/10 fun during n/a phase. Consider restricting mini-games to n/a phase.
Marcus only engaged with 27% of events. Consider targeting events to their interests or reducing frequency.
Verdict reveals were mentioned as shareable by 83% of agents. This is strong word-of-mouth potential.
Group bonding scored 8.3/10 -- the social glue is working. Preserve collaborative events.
